levels.name: Beginner
levels.description: New to structured fitness training. Learning fundamental movement patterns.

levels.name: Beginner+
levels.description: Basic movements mastered. Building consistency and work capacity.

levels.name: Intermediate
levels.description: Solid fundamentals across all categories. Handles moderate loads with good form.

levels.name: Intermediate+
levels.description: Competent athlete proficient across all fitness domains. Requires periodized programming.

levels.name: Advanced
levels.description: Strong and skilled across all categories. Competition-capable.

levels.name: Advanced+
levels.description: Very strong and highly skilled. Competition-level fitness across all domains.

levels.name: Rx
levels.description: Elite-level performance. Top 5% of trained athletes.

categories.name: Squatting
categories.description: Lower body pushing strength through squat patterns

categories.name: Pulling
categories.description: Hip-hinge and pulling strength from the floor

categories.name: Pressing
categories.description: Upper body pressing strength in all planes

categories.name: Olympic Lifting
categories.description: Speed-strength and technical proficiency in the Olympic lifts

categories.name: Gymnastics
categories.description: Bodyweight strength, control, and skill on apparatus

categories.name: Endurance
categories.description: Sustained effort across extended mixed-modal workouts. Benchmarks in this category originated in the CrossFit community and are used here by their widely recognized names.

sources.citation: van den Hoek, D.J., Beaumont, P.L., van den Hoek, A.K., Owen, P.J., Garrett, J.M., Buhmann, R., & Latella, C. (2024). Normative data for the squat, bench press and deadlift exercises in powerlifting: Data from 809,986 competition entries. Journal of Science and Medicine in Sport.
Mangine, G.T. et al. (2020). Determination of a CrossFit Benchmark Performance Profile. International Journal of Exercise Science.
Mangine, G.T., Grundlingh, N., & Feito, Y. (2023). Normative scores for CrossFit Open workouts: 2011-2022. Sports, 11(2), 24.
Kilgore, L. (2023). Lon Kilgore Strength Standard Tables.
Concept2 Logbook World Rankings (2025 season data).
RunningLevel.com race time standards. Based on 1,000,000+ race results.
ACSM's Guidelines for Exercise Testing and Prescription. American College of Sports Medicine.
Physical Fitness Specialist Certification Manual. The Cooper Institute for Aerobics Research, Dallas TX (1997).
U.S. Military Physical Fitness Test Standards. Department of Defense (public domain).
StrengthLevel.com exercise standards. Community-sourced performance data.
Everett, G. (2018). Olympic Weightlifting Skill Levels Chart. Catalyst Athletics.
Butcher, S.J. et al. (2018). Normative Values for Self-Reported Benchmark Workout Scores in CrossFit Practitioners. Sports Medicine - Open.
CrossFit Open Workout Analysis articles (various years). CrossFit Games.
CrossFit Liftoff percentile data (2015). CrossFit Games.
Rikli, R.E. & Jones, C.J. (2013). Senior Fitness Test Manual. 2nd Ed. Human Kinetics.
sources.notes: Defines the 30-second chair stand test. Used globally in clinical and rehab settings for functional fitness assessment in older adults and deconditioned populations.

sources.citation: Haff, G.G. & Triplett, N.T. (Eds.) (2016). Essentials of Strength Training and Conditioning. 4th Ed. National Strength and Conditioning Association. Human Kinetics.
sources.notes: Standard reference for exercise progression and regression principles, push-up progressions, and foundational movement patterns.

sources.citation: World Health Organization (2020). WHO Guidelines on Physical Activity and Sedentary Behaviour. Geneva: WHO.
sources.notes: Sets global minimum physical activity recommendations. Relevant for walk/run progression baseline thresholds.

sources.citation: McGill, S.M., Belore, M., Crosby, I., & Russell, C. (2015). Clinical tools to quantify torso flexion endurance: normative data from student and firefighter populations. Occupational Ergonomics, 12(2), 59-66.
sources.notes: Provides normative plank hold durations for healthy populations. Used to set Foundation Milestone core endurance criteria.

sources.citation: Bohannon, R.W. (2019). Grip Strength: An Indispensable Biomarker For Older Adults. Clinical Interventions in Aging, 14, 1681-1691.
sources.notes: Establishes grip strength and hanging capacity as functional health markers. Supports dead hang and farmer's carry as foundation assessments.

sources.citation: Chodzko-Zajko, W.J. et al. (2009). Exercise and Physical Activity for Older Adults. ACSM Position Stand. Medicine & Science in Sports & Exercise, 41(7), 1510-1530.
sources.notes: Foundational clinical guidelines for exercise prescription in older adults. Recommends low initial intensity with gradual progression.

sources.citation: Fragala, M.S. et al. (2019). Resistance Training for Older Adults: Position Statement From the National Strength and Conditioning Association. Journal of Strength and Conditioning Research, 33(8).
sources.notes: NSCA position statement on resistance training for older adults. Recommends starting with 1 set of 8-10 exercises at low intensity, progressing no more than once every 4 weeks.

sources.citation: Jones, C.J., Rikli, R.E., & Beam, W.C. (1999). A 30-s Chair-Stand Test as a Measure of Lower Body Strength in Community-Residing Older Adults. Research Quarterly for Exercise and Sport, 70(2), 113-119.
sources.notes: Validation study for the 30-second chair stand test. Test-retest reliability r=0.89. Normative data for ages 60-94 across 7,183 participants.

sources.citation: Strand, S.L., Hjelm, J., Shoepe, T.C., & Fajardo, M.A. (2014). Norms for an Isometric Muscle Endurance Test. Journal of Human Kinetics, 40, 93-102.
sources.notes: Normative plank hold and core endurance data for healthy adults. Provides percentile rankings for isometric endurance tests.

bodyweight.name: Push-ups
bodyweight.description: Maximum unbroken push-ups with full range of motion (chest to floor, full lockout)
bodyweight.notes: Upper bound of 199 indicates 'or more' for the top level.

bodyweight.name: Double-Unders
bodyweight.description: Maximum unbroken double-unders (jump rope passes twice per jump)
bodyweight.notes: Skill-based movement; standards are typically the same for male and female. Upper bound of 999 indicates 'or more' for the top level.

bodyweight.name: Pistol Squat
bodyweight.description: Maximum alternating pistol squats (single-leg squat to full depth, per leg)
bodyweight.notes: Reps per leg. Requires significant single-leg strength, balance, and ankle mobility. Upper bound of 99 indicates 'or more' for the top level.

endurance.name: Fran
endurance.description: 21-15-9 reps of thrusters (43/30kg) and pull-ups, for time

endurance.name: Murph
endurance.description: 1 mile run + 100 pull-ups + 200 push-ups + 300 air squats + 1 mile run, with weight vest (9/6kg)

endurance.name: Cindy
endurance.notes: Score in completed rounds. Higher is better. Each round = 5 pull-ups + 10 push-ups + 15 air squats.

gymnastics.name: Strict Pull-ups
gymnastics.description: Maximum unbroken strict pull-ups (dead hang, chin over bar)
gymnastics.notes: Upper bound of 99 indicates 'or more' for the top level.

gymnastics.name: Handstand Push-ups (Strict)
gymnastics.description: Maximum unbroken strict handstand push-ups (wall-facing or back-to-wall, head to floor)
gymnastics.notes: Strict HSPU requires significant pressing strength and balance. Upper bound of 99 indicates 'or more' for the top level.

gymnastics.name: Toes to Bar
gymnastics.description: Maximum unbroken toes-to-bar (kipping or strict, toes touch bar simultaneously)
gymnastics.notes: Upper bound of 99 indicates 'or more' for the top level.

gymnastics.name: Bar Muscle-up
gymnastics.description: Maximum unbroken bar muscle-ups (kipping or strict)
gymnastics.notes: High-skill movement requiring both pull-up and dip strength. Upper bound of 99 indicates 'or more' for the top level.

gymnastics.name: Ring Muscle-up
gymnastics.description: Maximum unbroken ring muscle-ups (kipping or strict)
gymnastics.notes: Most demanding gymnastics movement. Requires strong false grip and transition. Upper bound of 99 indicates 'or more' for the top level.

monostructural.name: 500m Row
monostructural.description: 500 meter row for time on Concept2 rower

monostructural.name: 2000m Row
monostructural.description: 2000 meter row for time on Concept2 rower

olympic_lifting.name: Power Clean
olympic_lifting.description: Maximum single repetition power clean (caught above parallel)
olympic_lifting.notes: Values based on ~80kg male / ~60kg female. Adjust proportionally for bodyweight.

olympic_lifting.name: Snatch
olympic_lifting.description: Maximum single repetition full snatch (squat snatch)
olympic_lifting.notes: Most technically demanding Olympic lift. Values based on ~80kg male / ~60kg female.

olympic_lifting.name: Clean & Jerk
olympic_lifting.description: Maximum single repetition clean and jerk (full clean, split or power jerk)
olympic_lifting.notes: Values based on ~80kg male / ~60kg female. Adjust proportionally for bodyweight.

pressing.name: Strict Press
pressing.description: Maximum single repetition strict shoulder press (standing, no leg drive)
pressing.notes: Values based on ~80kg male / ~60kg female. Adjust proportionally for bodyweight.

pressing.name: Bench Press
pressing.description: Maximum single repetition flat barbell bench press
pressing.notes: Values based on ~80kg male / ~60kg female. Adjust proportionally for bodyweight.

pulling.name: Deadlift
pulling.notes: Values based on ~80kg male / ~60kg female. Adjust proportionally for bodyweight.

squatting.name: Back Squat
squatting.notes: Values based on ~80kg male / ~60kg female. Adjust proportionally for bodyweight.

squatting.name: Front Squat
squatting.notes: Approximately 85% of back squat values. Based on ~80kg male / ~60kg female.

squatting.name: Overhead Squat
squatting.notes: Approximately 65% of back squat values. Requires significant shoulder and thoracic mobility. Based on ~80kg male / ~60kg female.
